subject: LU-11079 llite: control concurrent statahead instances

values.-1: I would prefer that you didn't submit this
values.+1: Looks good to me, but someone else must approve

messages.message: Uploaded patch set 1.

Patch Set 1:

Looks good to me.

Patch Set 1:

Code-wise looks good to me.

Patch Set 1: Code-Review+1

As long as this only stops statahead threads, but doesn't cause additional readdir() processes to fail, that is OK.

Patch Set 1:

> As long as this only stops statahead threads, but doesn't cause additional readdir() processes to fail, that is OK.

This patch only prevents too many concurrent statahead threads to avoid statahead RPCs flood, but it will NOT affect the correctness.
Uploaded patch set 2.

Patch Set 4:

Have you explored moving from threads to work queues to see if that helps?

Patch Set 4:

(1 comment)

> Have you explored moving from threads to work queues to see if that helps?
Not yet. But I am afraid whether the performance can be accepted or not since the work queue is shared.

On the other hand, statahead/AGL have very complex control logic. If we want to switch to work queue, some of the logic have to be well considered to avoid lock/RAM trouble.

parents.subject: LU-10893 tests: allow to disable dm-flakey layer

commit.subject: LU-11079 llite: control concurrent statahead instances
commit.message:
LU-11079 llite: control concurrent statahead instances

It is found that if there are too many concurrent statahead
instances, then related statahead RPCs may accumulate on the
client import (for MDT) RPC lists
(imp_sending_list/imp_delayed_list/imp_unreplied_lis), as to
seriously affect the efficiency of spin_lock under the case
of MDT overloaded or in recovery. Be as the temporarily solution,
restrict the concurrent statahead instances.

If want to support more concurrent statahead instances, please
consider to decentralize the RPC lists attached on related import.

Signed-off-by: Fan Yong <fan.yong@intel.com>
Change-Id: I7251cc536f11d184f768e3d3704ba6717644541e
Reviewed-on: https://review.whamcloud.com/32690
Tested-by: Jenkins
Reviewed-by: Andreas Dilger <adilger@whamcloud.com>
Tested-by: Maloo <hpdd-maloo@intel.com>
Reviewed-by: Lai Siyao <lai.siyao@whamcloud.com>
Reviewed-by: Oleg Drokin <green@whamcloud.com>
